---
title: ""
---

::: {.content-section}

# Teaching {.section-heading}

I teach undergraduate and graduate courses in environmental economics, natural resource economics, and quantitative methods at the University of Alberta.

Course materials — slides, problem sets, and announcements — are available to enrolled students via the [student portal](students/index.html).

```{=html}
<div class="course-grid" style="margin-top:2rem;">

  <div class="course-card">
    <div class="course-code">Econ 365</div>
    <div class="course-name">Natural Resource Economics</div>
    <div class="course-desc">Economics of non-renewable and renewable resources, extraction theory, resource policy, and sustainability.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="course-card">
    <div class="course-code">Econ 467</div>
    <div class="course-name">Environmental Economics</div>
    <div class="course-desc">Market failures, externalities, environmental regulation, cost-benefit analysis, and climate policy.</div>
  </div>

  <div class="course-card">
    <div class="course-code">Econ 566</div>
    <div class="course-name">Graduate Methods</div>
    <div class="course-desc">Graduate-level mathematics and statistics for economists.</div>
  </div>

</div>
```

:::
